content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:好的，朱莉娅，从你的简历和作品集来看，还有你已经告诉我的。你似乎正是我们研究生课程要找的那种人。告诉我，你四年前在伦敦完成了时装设计课程，是吗
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Right Julia, so from your CV and portfolio, and what you've already told me.you seem to be very much the sort of person we're looking for on the postgraduate course. So tell me, you finished your Fashion Design course in London four years ago did

content.cn_text: 你当时想过继续深造，获得更高的学位吗?
content.en_text: you think of carrying straight on and doing a higher degree at the time?

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:对，不过有经济压力。所以我最终在零售行业工作，你可以从我的简历中看到。实际上这是一次非常有用的经历。
content.en_text: Julia: Yes but there were financial pressures. So I ended up working in the retail industry, as you can see from my CV. And actually it was a very useful experience.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:嗯，怎么说?
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Mmm Iri what way?

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:嗯，我很幸运能在FashionNow得到这份工作，他们是一家大商店，我的首要任务之一就是在不同的领域获得尽可能多的经验，这很好，因为我有机会在很多不同的部门工作。与顾客直接接触意味着我能够看到他们对创新的反应——对新的时尚理念的反应，因为有了FashionNow，设计师可能会在纽约或米兰展示一些东西，几周内就会在店里推出类似的东西。所以，这可能是对我最有用的东西。
content.en_text: Julia: Well, I was lucky to get the job with FashionNow - they're a big store, and, one of my priorities was to get as much experience as possible in different areas, so that was good because I had the chance to work in lots of different departments. And having direct contact with the customers meant I was able to see how they reacted to innovation - to new fashion ideas, because with FashionNow, a designer might show something in New York or Milan and there'll be something similar in the shop within weeks. So, that was probably the most useful thing for me.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:对。那么是什么让你现在决定攻读研究生课程呢?
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Right. And so what's made you decide to do a postgraduate course now?

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:嗯。虽然我很喜欢在FashionNow工作，也学到了很多东西，但我觉得……嗯，未来的发展方向是发展我的管理技能，而不是时装设计技能，我不确定那是不是我想做的。
content.en_text: Julia: Erm . Well, while I enjoyed working at FashionNow and I learned a lot there, I felt.. well, the way forward would have been to develop my managerial skills rather than my skills in fashion design, and I'm not sure that's what I want to do.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:嗯，是的。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Mmm, yes.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:我在伦敦读学位的时候对女装很感兴趣。但要知道，在新面料结构等领域已经做了很多工作——尽管我不打算太深入地研究这项技术，但我对如何将新面料应用于童装非常感兴趣，所以我希望有机会从事这一领域的研究。
content.en_text: Julia: When I was doing my degree in London I'd been interested in women's wear. But know that there's been a lot of work done in areas like new fabric construction - and though I'm not intending to go too deeply into the technology I'd be very interested in looking at how new fabrics could be used in children's wear, so I'd like the chance to pursue that line.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:是的。好。在离开学术环境几年之后，你是否担心回到学术环境会是什么样子?
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Yes. Good. And are you at all concerned about what's going to be like coming back into an academic context after being away from it for several years?

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:不，我很期待。但我基本上对应用更感兴趣，而不是理论，至少我到目前为止是这样发现的，我希望这门课程能给我提供建立自己企业所需的人脉和技能。我对该部门的海外联系特别感兴趣。
content.en_text: Julia: No, I'm looking forward to it. But I'm basically more interested in the application than the theory or at least that's what I've found so far, and I'm hoping the course will give me the contacts and skills I need eventually to set up my own enterprise I'm particularly interested by the overseas links that the department has.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:是的，我们的许多学生都把目光投向海外或国际公司，寻求他们项目的赞助。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Yes, many of our students look overseas or to international companies for sponsorship of their projects.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:而且这里的设施看起来很棒。我刚去图书馆看了看，真是令人印象深刻。和我以前的大学相比，这里的房间多了。
content.en_text: Julia: And the facilities here look excellent. I just went to look at the library - it's really impressive. There's so much room compared with the one at my old university.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:是的，大多数学生认为这是一个学习的好地方。当然，还有与其他大学的联系，所有常见的电子资源，工作人员都有一个信息技能课程，我们建议所有研究生在第一周或两周内完成。设计专业的学生发现特别收藏特别有用。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Yes, most students find it's a good place to study. And there are linkups to other universities, of course, and all the usual electronic sources the staff run an Information Skills Programme which we recommend all postgraduates do in the first week or two. Design students find the Special Collections particularly useful.

content.cn_text: 茱莉亚:是的。
content.en_text: Julia: Yes.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:我们还有一个独立的计算机中心，它有自己的学术协调员蒂姆·斯彭德(Tim Spender)，他有艺术设计的背景，该中心的宗旨是它是一个创新和创造力的工作室，而不是一个计算机实验室。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Then we have a separate Computer Centre, which has its own academic coordinator, Tim Spender - he's got a background in art design, and the ethos of the centre is that it's a studio for innovation and creativity, rather than a computer laboratory.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:哦，对，我喜欢那里的学习空间，学生们可以坐在那里一起讨论工作，这对合作项目很有用。我读本科的时候，我们总是在食堂里做这种事情。我在宣传册上看到有一个单独的摄影资源。
content.en_text: Julia: Oh, right, i liked the study spaces where students can sit and discuss work together very useful for joint projects We always had to do that sort of thing in the cafeteria when l was an undergraduate. And I read in the brochure that there's a separate resource for photography.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:是的，它叫做photommedia。它不仅适用于摄影，还适用于数字成像和新媒体。这是我们所有学生的资源，而不仅仅是时装设计，我们鼓励学生在那里工作，创造跨越学科界限的作品。事实上，自从三年前建立以来，它的规模已经翻了一番。我们还有一个分支叫做基于时间的媒体，这是为那些想要在运动图像或声音领域发展自己想法的学生准备的。那是在的一座新大楼，是去年专门为它建造的，但由于目前的设施已经不堪重负，因此有计划对其进行扩建。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Yes, it's called Photomedia. It's not just for photography, but things like digital imaging and new media. It's a resource for all our students, not just fashion design,and we encourage students to work there producing work that crosses disciplinary boundaries. It's well used in fact, it's doubled in size since it was set up three years ago.And we also have an offshoot from that which is called Time Based Media - this is for students who want to develop their ideas in the area of the moving image or sound.That's in  a new building that was specially built for it just last year, but there are plans to expand it as the present facilities are overstretched already.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:对的。
content.en_text: Julia: Right.

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:现在。你对课程本身有什么问题要问的吗?
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Now. is there anything you'd like to ask about the course itself?

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:嗯，……我知道这是教学模块和专业项目的结合，但评估是如何发挥作用的呢?
content.en_text: Julia: Erm, ... I know it's a combination of taught modules and a specialist project, but how does assessment lit in?

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:嗯，正如你所预料的，在这种性质的课程中，这是一个持续的过程。学位课程分为四个阶段，在前三个阶段的最后都有我们所说的进度回顾。最后的评估是基于你的项目。你必须写一份报告，对你的工作进行批判性的反思。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: Well, as you'd expect on a course of this nature, it's an ongoing process.The degree course has four stages, and there are what we call progress reviews at the end of each of the first three. Then the final assessment is based on your project. You have to produce a report which is a  critical reflection on your work.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:会有时装表演吗?
content.en_text: Julia: And is there some sort of fashion show?

content.cn_text: 希尔斯登博士:有一个展览。这些项目并不都专注于服装本身，有些项目更具实验性，所以这样做似乎更合适。我们邀请了时尚公司的代表一起参加，通常出席人数很多。
content.en_text: Dr Hilsden: There's an exhibition. The projects aren't all focused on clothes as such, some are more experimental, so that seems more appropriate. We ask representatives of fashion companies along, and it's usually well attended.

content.cn_text: 朱莉娅:对的。我还想问你一件事。
content.en_text: Julia: Right. And another thing I wanted to ask .

groups.desc:
Questions 21-23
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What is the main thing Julia feels she has gained from her experience in retail?

options.title: B
options.content: improved ability to predict fashion trends

options.title: C
options.content: more skill in setting priorities in her work

items.analysis: Julia明确表示，与顾客直接接触让她能够观察到顾客对新时尚理念的反应，并强调这是她在零售经验中获得的最有用的东西。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"更好地理解顾客态度"。Julia强调了这种直接接触和观察的重要性，而不是预测趋势或设定优先级的能力，因此A选项最符合原文内容。

items.title: Why is Julia interested in doing the postgraduate course?

options.title: A
options.content: It will enable her to develop new types of technology.

options.title: B
options.content: It will allow her to specialise in a design area of her choice.

options.title: C
options.content: It will provide managerial training focusing on her needs

items.analysis: Julia明确表示她对研究新面料在童装中的应用非常感兴趣，并希望能够继续这个方向的研究。这表明她选择这个研究生课程是为了能够专攻她所选择的设计领域（童装），这与选项B完全吻合。此外，她提到了从女装到童装的兴趣转变，这进一步证实了她想要在特定设计领域深入研究的意愿。

items.title: What would Julia like to do after she has completed the postgraduate course?
items.analysis: Julia明确表示她希望通过这个研究生课程获得所需的人脉和技能，最终目的是创办自己的企业。"set up my own enterprise"直接对应了选项B "start her own business"。这表明Julia在完成课程后的长期目标是创业，而不是在学术环境中继续发展或单纯地在海外工作。

groups.desc:
Questions 24-27
What does each university facility have?
Choose your answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G next to
Questions 24-27.

items.title: Library
items.analysis: 图书馆提供了一个入门课程（introductory course）是正确答案，因为原文中提到图书馆工作人员开设了一个"Information Skills Programme"，这是一个为新生设计的入门课程。此外，文中特别强调建议所有研究生在入学初期参加这个课程，进一步证实了这是一个入门性质的课程。

items.title: Computer Centre
items.analysis: 文中明确指出计算机中心被描述为"a studio for innovation and creativity"，而不是一个普通的计算机实验室。这直接对应了正确答案G "emphasis on creative use"。中心的重点在于促进创新和创造力，而不仅仅是提供计算机设备。这种描述突出了该设施对创意使用的强调，完全符合选项G的内容。

items.title: Photomedia
items.analysis: 文中明确提到Photomedia是"面向所有学生的资源，不仅仅是时尚设计专业"，并且鼓励学生进行"跨越学科界限的创作"。这直接表明了Photomedia具有跨学科的特点，与选项C "inter-disciplinary focus"（跨学科焦点）完全吻合，因此C是正确答案。

items.title: Time Based Media
items.analysis: 文中明确提到Time Based Media位于"a new building that was specially built for it just last year"（去年专门为其建造的新建筑），这直接对应了选项E "purpose-built premises"（专门建造的场所）。"specially built"和"new building"都强调了这个设施是为特定目的而新建的，完全符合"purpose-built premises"的定义。因此，E是最准确的答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 28-30
Complete the summary below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
MA in Fashion Design: Assessment
Assessment includes three $15837$ which take place at the end of the stages of the degree. Final assessment is based on a project, and includes the student's $15838$ in the form of a written report, and the $15839$ to which representatives of fashion companies are invited.

items.analysis:
这道题的关键在于理解评估方式。文中明确提到了"progress reviews"这个词，它直接对应了题目中所问的评估方式。让我们来分析一下为什么这是正确答案：

1. 首先，文中提到学位课程有四个阶段，这与题目中提到的"stages of the degree"相对应。

2. 其次，讲话者使用了"what we call"这个短语来引出"progress reviews"这个术语，这表明这是他们用来描述评估方式的专门用语。

3. 最后，文中明确指出这些"progress reviews"发生在前三个阶段的结束时，这与题目中要求的"take place at the end of the stages"完全吻合。

因此，"progress reviews"是最准确的答案。它既符合题目要求的评估性质，又符合发生的时间和频率。记住，在听力考试中，有时候关键信息会以专业术语的形式出现，所以要特别留意像"what we call"这样的引导短语，它们往往会引出重要的答案信息。

这道题的正确答案是"(critical) reflection"，因为原文中明确提到"You have to produce a report which is a critical reflection on your work"。这里，"critical reflection"就是你需要在报告中完成的内容。题目要求填写的是学生需要在书面报告中提供的内容，而"critical reflection"正是这个报告的核心。

需要注意的是，题目中提到"written report"，这与原文中的"produce a report"相对应。同时，"Final assessment"和"based on a project"这两个关键信息也在原文中得到了确认。这些对应关系帮助我们确定了答案的位置。

另外，虽然文中也提到了"progress reviews"，但这不是最终评估的内容，而是在前三个阶段结束时进行的，因此不是正确答案。学生在听力过程中需要注意区分这些不同类型的评估。

总的来说，这道题考察了学生对学术评估过程的理解，以及对关键信息的捕捉能力。正确回答这个问题需要学生能够准确理解"critical reflection"这一学术术语，并将其与题目要求的"written report"内容联系起来。

同学，这道题的正确答案是"exhibition"。让我来解释一下为什么：

首先，题干中提到了"Final assessment"和"representatives of fashion companies"，这两个关键词帮助我们在原文中定位到相关信息。在对话中，当Julia问到是否有时装秀时，Dr Hilsden明确回答说有一个exhibition（展览）。

Dr Hilsden解释说，展览比时装秀更合适，因为学生的项目不仅仅局限于服装设计，有些项目更具实验性。这表明exhibition更能全面展示学生的作品。

此外，Dr Hilsden提到他们邀请时尚公司的代表参加这个展览，这正好对应了题干中的"representatives of fashion companies are invited"。

所以，"exhibition"不仅符合题干的要求，也最准确地反映了原文中描述的最终评估形式。记住，在听力考试中，我们需要根据原文信息回答，而不是根据我们的常识或猜测。这里，虽然时装设计课程通常会有时装秀，但原文明确表示他们选择了展览这种形式。
